        print(f"Row {i}: {', '.join(changes)}")

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the right green U-shape (node 14) in the double container RESTORED the single green container to its original state! The right half of the N shape came back, and the red dot moved back from (10,29) to (10,31).
So the pattern is:
- Clicking on the left green U-shape (node 13) in the double container: removes the right half of the N shape and moves the red dot left
- Clicking on the right green U-shape (node 14) in the double container: restores the right half and moves the red dot back right
This is like a toggle or a swap mechanism. The two U-shapes in the double container control the state of the single container.
Now let me think about the goal:
- There are two single containers (green and yellow) and two double containers (green and yellow)
- Each double container has two U-shapes that can be clicked
- Clicking on these U-shapes affects the corresponding single container
- The red cross markers might be target positions
The goal might be to:
1. Move the red dots to match the cross marker positions
2. Or to make the single containers match some target configuration
Let me try clicking on the yellow U-shapes to see if they affect the yellow single container similarly.
But first, let me understand the current state:
- The single green container is back to its original state (full N shape with red dot at center)
- The red dot at (10,31) in the green container
- The red dot at (34,10) in the yellow container
The cross markers are at:
- Top-right: around (10,52)
- Bottom-left: around (52,10)
Hmm, maybe the goal is to move the red dots to the cross marker positions? That seems far away. Or maybe the goal is to match the U-shapes in the double containers with the single containers.
Let me try clicking on the yellow U-shapes to see what happens.
